attacking_style: Breakaway specialist who commits early on long, steady climbs rather than explosive final ascent attacks. Struggles to finish when gradients exceed 2000m altitude or require punchy accelerations in final kilometers.
team_tactics: Deployed as expendable domestique to support protected climbers (Chaves), willing to be sacrificed before final climbs to set tempo or test rivals. Used in breakaway reconnaissance rather than GC protection.
terrain_preference: Long, sustained alpine climbs with steady gradients (e.g., Izoard) where endurance trumps explosive power. Exposed on technical descents, first-bend positioning, and high-altitude finishes above 2000m requiring repeated accelerations.
vulnerability: Poor bike handling on technical courses causing crashes in pressured situations. Lacks finishing punch on steep final ascents after bridging efforts. Cannot follow explosive accelerations at high altitude.
key_patterns: Dropped on final steep ascents after bridging/pacing efforts; lacks finishing punch (2 instances)
Strong in long breakaways on steady climbs until final kilometer pressure applied (2 instances)
Technical/handling weaknesses: crashes and positioning errors in bunch scenarios (2 instances)
Used as expendable support rider rather than protected climber in Grand Tours (2 instances)
form_trajectory: negative: Crash liability early in Vuelta, then repeatedly dropped on final climbs despite bridging capacity, suggests declining explosiveness through August 2017
